report_metadata.report_title: The five-day job: A BlackByte ransomware intrusion case study

ip_addresses.evidence_snippet: The threat actor was observed operating from the following IP to exploit ProxyShell and access the web shell: 185.225.73[.]244
This beacon was configured to communicate with the following C2 channel: 109.206.243[.]59:443

domains.evidence_snippet: popular file sharing platform Mega NZ using the platform’s API at: hxxps://g.api.mega.co[.]nz

file_hashes.evidence_snippet: rENEgOtiAtES (Vulnerable driver RtCore64.sys created by BlackByte binary)
C:SystemDataM8yl89s7.exe (UPX-packed PsExec with a random name; SHA-256: ba3ec3f445683d0d0407157fda0c26fd669c0b8cc03f21770285a20b3133098f)

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.evidence_snippet: AnyDesk Enables remote access to network devices for management, bypassing security alerts due to AnyDesk being a legitimate application.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike Beacon
tools.evidence_snippet: The threat actor leveraged Cobalt Strike to achieve persistence.

tools.name: Mimikatx
tools.evidence_snippet: Evidence of likely usage of the credential theft tool Mimikatzwas also uncovered

cves.evidence_snippet: exploiting the ProxyShell vulnerabilities CVE-2021-34473, CVE2021-34523, and CVE-2021-31207 on unpatched Microsoft Exchange Servers
exploiting the ProxyShell vulnerabilities CVE-2021-34473, CVE2021-34523, and CVE-2021-31207 on unpatched Microsoft Exchange Servers
exploiting the ProxyShell vulnerabilities CVE-2021-34473, CVE2021-34523, and CVE-2021-31207 on unpatched Microsoft Exchange Servers
C:SystemDatarENEgOtiAtES (A vulnerable (CVE-2019-16098) driver RtCore64.sys used to evade detection by installed antivirus software

ttps.technique: Exploitation of unpatched Microsoft Exchange Servers
ttps.evidence_snippet: CVE-2021-34473, CVE2021-34523, and CVE-2021-31207 on unpatched Microsoft Exchange Servers.

ttps.technique: Impersonation via ProxyShell exploitation and web shells
ttps.evidence_snippet: Impersonate domain admin users and create a web shell by using the
created the following registry run keys to run a payload

ttps.technique: Network enumeration with NetScan and AdFind
ttps.evidence_snippet: presence and execution of the network discovery tool NetScan
Evidence of likely usage of the credential theft tool Mimikatz

ttps.technique: RDP and PowerShell remoting with compromised credentials
ttps.evidence_snippet: used Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) and PowerShell remoting to obtain access to other servers

ttps.technique: Data staging and exfiltration with ExByte to Mega NZ
ttps.evidence_snippet: Mega NZ using the platform’s API at: hxxps://g.api.mega.co[.]nz
Process hollowing rENEgOtiAtES created matches RTCore64.sys, a vulnerable driver (CVE-2019-16098)

ttps.technique: Disable Windows Firewall and shadow copies
ttps.evidence_snippet: netsh advfirewall set allprofiles state off vssadmin Resize ShadowStorage

summary: Case study of BlackByte ransomware intrusion exploiting ProxyShell on Exchange servers, deploying web shells, backdoors (api-msvc.dll), Cobalt Strike, AnyDesk, NetScan, AdFind, Mimikatz, ExByte exfiltration tool, and BlackByte 2.0 ransomware within five days. Includes IOCs like IPs, hashes, C2 domains.

timeline.event: Publication of BlackByte ransomware case study
timeline.evidence_snippet: The five-day job: A BlackByte ransomware intrusion case study July 6, 2023
